fence...and people wont notice you are actually supporting an
oppressive state.

Is it any wonder why America is being targeted by terrorists when
America gives around 50% $Billions of its annual foreign aid to this
country that has one of the worst human rights records on the planet.

Fact!...The real reason America supports Israel is because the
Evangelical Christian Movements belief is that Israels existence is
clearing the way for the second coming of Jesus in the holy land..
Politicians want their votes because they are a huge percentage of
voters in America, and upsetting this group would mean risking loosing
the presidency....it all comes down to supporting whoever wins you the
election and nothing what so ever to do with supporting human rights
and justice in the world..

To the passerby it seems as though the Palestine/Israel conflict is a
conflict of two sides.......at least that's the way the media often
portrays it. This is true to some extent....you can always find war
criminals on both sides of any world conflict.....I myself have in the
past lived and worked in many parts of the west bank, Palestine, and
Israel for a number of years and felt compelled to write this article
for the benefit of all those concerned.

The reason i am posting this letter is that i hope somebody of
influence reading it will take my suggestion and act on it.

There is no doubt that when watching the news on the conflict most
viewers are left with the impression "It's a war of two
sides"......and this is the problem......giving the general public a
TRUE picture of what is happening is impossible due to both sides
continually playing the blame game in the media refusing to see the
sufferings of the other side.....result:...people are left with the
impression, "I guess they are probably as bad as each other"...This
statement is not completely untrue....nobody can justify the murder of
Israeli children by the use of a suicide bomber nor can Israel justify
the state murder of hundreds of Palestinian children each year and call
it self defense against terrorism. Like I said before, there are always
war crimes on both sides of every conflict. But what stands out the
most about this conflict is the disturbingly unbalanced body count
figures published annually by the UN. Year after year the Israeli
military is killing 4 times more people than casualtys caused by
Palestinian violence.

Instead of Palestinian organizations putting up posters and flyers
stating "apartied - bring down the wall etc"......why not focus PR
campaigns to include the most UNARGUABLE FACTS!.....This and only this
will turn heads and change public opinion for the better...forget the
injustices and sentiment for a minute and focus on what will win the
hearts and minds of the masses...UNARGUABLE FACTS that relate to the
values of all people.

I am not up to date with the latest body count figures but i am
informed that UN recent figures state a ratio of aprox 3-5 dead
Palestinians to every Israeli, and year after year this ratio
continues.

When you consider that Israel is as well armed technologically as
the Americans, and that the Palestinians are....well lets just
say....slingshots made out of coca cola cans and the odd AK47 and home
made mortar don't do too much damage to a squadron of apache
helicopter gunships supplied by America.

With the above in mind, a rational person will tell you that if two
Unequally armed armies clash in a battle: The WORST CASE SENARIO is
that the most well armed side will suffer equal losses to that which it
inflicts....In such a scenario, at best a case of "SELF DEFENCE" and
"Minimum Force Necessary" might be claimed...but this scenario is
very rare and unlikely in warfare and only comes about when the
dominant army makes a serious strategic error.

If you found an adult dressed in armour holding high tech weapons
standing in a room with four dead children with pea shooters laying at
his feet and he told you he was forced to defend himself...would you
accept this?

When Israel is killing Palestinians at a ratio of approximately for
times as many and they are far better armed how can they seriously
claim self defense against terrorism???.....I was always taught that in
order to take the moral high ground and claim self defense you must use
the "Minimum Force Necessary"..Do these figures speak "Minimum
Force Necessary"???

In the civilized world, when a government is faced with a hostile
force the police are called in to form a barricade, if this proves
insufficient to keep the peace water cannons are used, then onto tear
gas, and if this still is not enough to control rioters the police may
use rubber bullets to disable there targets. In the extreme case police
may resort to live bullets and shoot at the legs...and in the extremely
rare case when this may fail to stop the most determined
opponent...shoot to kill if life is directly threatened. (This is the
definition of "Minimum force Necessary"

In Israel when a Palestinian attack takes place,  the Israeli Policy
is to punish all  Palestinians instead of targeting the few
responsible. All Palestinian towns and villages are put under curfew
and soldiers are given orders to shoot anybody seen walking the
streets. The familys of those responsible for attacks will have their
homes bulldozed as a deterent. As well as this the Israeli military
orders strikes with casualty figures in excess of those received and in
turn the Palestinians return a strike and the cycle of violence
continues with both sides making statements like "our attack was in
response to their attack". Which side is acting in self defence to an
attack received depends on your perspective.

In one recent year there were around 400 Israelis killed by Palestinian
militants. This is indeed a tragedy, however it is dwarfed by the fact
that an equal number of Palestinians were killed by Israeli forces in
the same period all of whom were children under the age of ten.
(categorized as terrorists by Isreal) and guilty of nothing more than
throwing rocks at Israeli soldiers.

I'm not sure how to put the above argument all in one nicely
designed poster and flyer, but if you can I think it would have a real
impact, my proposal is this:

1.	State UNARGUABLE unbiased reputable source figures (like the UN),

2.	State a worst case scenario argument.
3.	Close with the question: "Are these the figures of a country in self
defense against terrorism or is this genocide?"

The above ingrediance address the issues in a simplistic easy to
understand way for those with no background knowledge of the conflict
and also leaves little room for a counter argument response from pro
Israel groups. The truth always hurts the most.

Stating Logic & Truth, and backing it up with UN Statistics is the
way to turn heads.....post it up in every tube station and rail station
north to south, and give out thousands of flyers with this argument in
print ....i believe it will not fail to win the hearts and minds of the
masses.

I am not in contact with any pro Palestinian organizations but would
much appreciate it if readers can forward this idea to every campaign
office they know and also to the appropriate local and national
government office and any media/magazine/newsgroup address you can get
your hands on....i would love to see it posted from north to south.

To any Americans reading this article, you should be aware that your
government supports the state of Israel with billions of dollars of aid
each year ( approximately 50% of your foreign aid budget diverted from
people truly in need) because it is claimed "Israel is the only
democracy in the middle east", Israel is a democracy only by name but
far away from it in nature, the figures speak the truth. Please write a
letter to your senator and president asking him if these are the
figures of a democratic government in the Middle East, and hopefully
with a change in America's policy towards the middle east we can
start to see a reduction in the feelings of resentment towards America
in the muslim world.

When the world trade center was targeted on September 11th, America
was told the reason for it was that "These terrorists are fighting
against freedom itself", Ask yourself this.....If you were to
interview any of the Sept 11th bombers do you really believe they would
tell you they hate freedom?, is there anybody in the world who will
tell you they hate freedom?....No is the simple answer.....Or is it
more likely that these people or members of there family have in
someway been victims of Americas middle east policy's which have
filled them with misguided resentment and hate towards all
Americans?...........Your government would prefer you to think that
these people just wake up in the morning and feel like killing
civilians for no reason other than they hate people being free.

In my travels across the middle east prior to Sept 11th , I had the
opportunity to ask many muslims why they are so angry towards America,
the response was always the same again and again.....ISRAEL IS
OPPRESSING OUR FELLOW MUSLIMS!..and America is supporting their human
rights abuses with financial and military support........

For Fifty years now the United Nations has declared that Israel has
stolen and is illegally occupying the Palestinians land and that
Jerusalem is an illegally occupied city and the vast majority of
countries around the world support this view and call for Israel to
withdraw itself from Palestinian land year after year (accept America).
The American government has been ignoring this international ruling by
the UN for 50 years in a row despite the fact that the largest human
rights organization in the world Amnesty international condemns Israel
every year as having one of the worst human rights records on the
planet. America turns a blind eye again, fifty years in a row now......

After the Sept 11th terrorist attack the American government chose
to solve this age old dispute by launching bombs, and in the process
has killed dozens of thousands of Iraqi soldiers who were simply
obeying orders to defend there country, as would any American. And
today the body count has reached over 1000 American soldiers killed and
rising fast, just under 6000 injured, and of course you have at least
another ten thousand dead Iraqi civilians to date and that's after
the major assault on Iraq had ended.

When confronted by any problem, there are always two ways to solve
it..The intelligent way or the primitive impulsive monkey way

1)	With brute force and aggression (launch operation shock and ore on
Iraq and Afghanistan)...forgetting the philosophy that every action has
a reaction and sending the message America is not taking any
responsibility for its middle east policys of support for Israel and
infuriating the muslim world even more and setting America up for even
more future Sept 11th bombings and helping terrorist organizations to
recruit many more fighters.
2)	Or the intelligent way, which is to look at the root cause of why
your attacker is angry and if necessary defuse the problem from its
source as well as stopping the cycle of violence.

Sadly George Bush chose the impulsive way. And has only made American
citizens even more vulnerable. How many people do you think there are
at this very moment plotting to strike American soil because of the
attack on Iraq?, The answer is plenty more than there were before Sept
11th, some will be stopped, but as in the case of Israels lesson.
It's impossible to stop them all. Especially when the bombers are
prepared to die to reach their targets.

If America is a truly democratic country, then it should respect the
views of the UN and the vast majority of the world in regard to
Israel....The world has voted in regard to Israel, Its time to listen
to the result.

IS THERE A LINK BETWEEN TERRORISM IN THE USA AND PALESTINE???

READ THE BIN LADEN QUOTES BELOW TAKEN FROM AL QAEDAS LATEST VIDEO TAPE
RELEASE SEEN ON CNN AND BBC NEWS (OCTOBER 2004) AND DECIDE FOR
YOURSELF:

"I tell you that security is a major pillar of human life. Free men
do not renounce their security, irrespective of (US President George W)
Bush's claims that we hate freedom"

"I swear we never thought of attacking the towers, but when we saw
the injustice and arbitrariness of the US-Israeli alliance against our
brethren in Palestine and Lebanon, it became too much and the idea came
to me."

"The events that affected me directly go back to 1982 ... when
America gave the Israelis the green light to invade Lebanon, with the
backing of the US Sixth Fleet. It is difficult to describe what I felt
in these painful moments, but it created an overwhelming feeling of
refusal of injustice and a compelling determination to punish the
unjust. As I watched the destroyed towers in Lebanon, it occurred to me
that the unjust should suffer the same, that the towers in America must
be destroyed so that America gets a taste of what we went through, so
that it will stop killing our children and women"

Your security is not in the hands of (Democratic presidential
candidate) John Kerry, Bush or Al-Qaeda. Your security is in your own
hands. Any (presidential) mandate which does not play havoc with our
security would automatically ensure its own security."

PLEASE NOTE: I in no way condone the actions of September the 11th or
Palestinian attacks on civilians.....this article was written to point
out the huge imbalance of acts of violence and Americas miss guided
middle east policy and the consequences it has brought on the world.

A FINAL NOTE:......The Israeli position in regards to peace is
"surrender to our will and occupation or its war" Israel believes
that the Jews have a religious right to take over Palestinian land
because the Jews occupied this piece of land some 2000 years ago. If
the world were to operate around these rules the British would have a
right to invade and take back America because the British used to
occupy America only a few hundred years ago...and of course would
punish any American terrorists who have a problem with that. The
Israeli government say they are all for peace negotiations and are
prepared to talk about withdrawal from Palestinian land. At the same
time, the government is spending millions building new Israeli housing
settlements on Palestinian land.....If Israel has the intention to
withdraw...would they still be spending millions on new building
developments only to have to abandon them later???....I think this is
what George Bush would call a "no brainer". Israel Has built these
settlements because they can use this as a bargaining chip in
negotiating a settlement...... saying it is not reasonable to ask so
many Israelis to leave their new homes. Plain and simply it is an
illegal land grab and has been ruled illegal internationally.

The Palestinian position is the request that the UN resolutions that
have already been voted for by the international community are upheld
and the Israeli government be forced by UN sanctions to withdraw their
troops back into their own borders and land given to them by the UN
(Is this not a peaceful and reasonable request when all Palestinians
are asking for is that international law and voting be upheld?)
.......Once again like with the Iraq war the American government has
ignored these UN rulings and only follows the rules of the UN when they
feel like it....A very dangerous example to set for the international
community. Why should other countries obey the UN when those with great
power don't?

If you have mass mailing software please put it to good use and mass
mail the Christian evangelical movement, pro Palestine campaign groups,
government lobbying groups, and media. I give my full permission for
this article to be published and distributed free of charge for both
profit or campaigning reasons.

Regards.......A.B
```

On Wed, 05 Jan 2005 14:39:31 -0500, "Dub"
<scott.robbs@firstextended.com> wrote:

>I see the copybook, but not a delemited file of it, I also don't see a data
>file.  Also what does it take to get complete copies of the programs, as
>advertized this is what I need.

The delimited file is shown right after the copybook. An input data
file can easily be made with your text editor; columns for the fields
are shown on the sample report. The complete program is in Google's
archive. Get it in your browser and do a SaveAs.

http://groups-beta.google.com/group/comp.lang.cobol/browse_thread/thread/dc52411acc35c5c0/41ceca276724555f
```

<a id="t-bd5ad22c1b"></a>
## Thread: *OLD* (can we say "ancient"?) COBOL Manuals — 1 msgs

### *OLD* (can we say "ancient"?) COBOL Manuals

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2005-01-05T21:14:53+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<hNYCd.238759$O24.40906@news.easynews.com>`

```
From one of the other lists that I follow, I found out about some "scanned" (PDF 
format) "ancient" computer manuals - including some "historic" COBOL ones.

  http://www.bitsavers.org/pdf/Index.txt

        includes the "entire" index - but included in it are:

dec/pdp10/DEC-10-KC1B-D_COBOL_Jul70.pdf    PDP-10 COBOL Programmers Ref Man (Jul 
1970)

ibm/14xx/C24-3242-2_dskCobolOp_Feb65.pdf       COBOL (on Tape) Specifications 
IBM 1401, 1440, and 1460

ibm/14xx/C24-1492-2_1401_tapeCobol.pdf             COBOL (on Tape) 
Specifications IBM 1401

ibm/14xx/C24-3146-3_1401_cobolOper.pdf             COBOL (on Tape) Operating 
Procedures IBM 1401 (1964)

ibm/14xx/C28-0327-1_1410cobol.pdf                        IBM 1410/7010 Operating 
System (1410-PR-155) COBOL (Nov 1963)

ibm/14xx/C28-0327-5_1410_Cobol.pdf                    IBM 1410/7010 Operating 
System (1410-PR-155) COBOL - 1410-CB-969 (Jul 1966)

ibm/7090/C28-6391-0_v13_COBOL.pdf                  IBM 7090/7094 IBSYS Operating 
System Version 13 COBOL Language

ibm/7090/J28-6260-1_7090_COBOL_Sep64.pdf     COBOL Language: Preliminary 
Specifications (Sep 1964)
```

I need to out put to a report a constant followed by a text field 
followed by a constant.  So if that text field has trailing spaces i 
must remove them.  In other languages that is an easy task (see my note 
below).  How do I acomplish that in Cobol?

Example output:
XabcdefX
XsdfasfasdftX
XkjhX

Jim

I'm working in MicroFocus Cobol.

Donald Tees wrote:
> Rover wrote:
> 
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…

```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trim equivilant

- **From:** l.willms@jpberlin.de (Lueko Willms)
- **Date:** 2005-01-06T14:37:00+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9OMMq7xuflB@jpberlin-l.willms.jpberlin.de>`
- **References:** `<JKSdnQ34P4P1pkDcRVn-qw@massilloncabletv.com> <XcbDd.14271$7n1.950640@news20.bellglobal.com> <xLidnYbV_8L1zUDcRVn-pg@massilloncabletv.com>`

```
.    On  06.01.05
  wrote  rover44614@RemoveThisStuffNetscape.net (Rover)
     on  /COMP/LANG/COBOL
     in  xLidnYbV_8L1zUDcRVn-pg@massilloncabletv.com
  about  Re: Trim equivilant


r> I need to out put to a report a constant followed by a text field
r> followed by a constant.  So if that text field has trailing spaces i
r> must remove them.  In other languages that is an easy task (see my
r> note below).  How do I acomplish that in Cobol?

    by using the STRING statement with the DELIMITED BY SPACE clause.

    like this:

    STRING
       Constant-1 DELIMITED BY SIZE
       Textfield-1 DELIMITED BY SPACE
       Constant-2 DELIMIED BY SIZE
           INTO receiving-field


    But beware: this would cut Textfield-1 at the FIRST space. If Only  
trailing spaces have to be removed, but possible spaces before to be  
preserved, then you would have to INSPECT Textfield-1 and then use  
reference modification, like this:

     Textfield-1 (1:Position-of-last-non-space-character)
        DELIMITED BY SIZE

   On various methods to scan for trailing spaces, search this  
newsgroup for several discussions on that. You might use keywords  
"INSPECT PERFORM VARYING REVERSE" and author name "Chuck Stevens".


Yours,
Lï¿½ko Willms                                     http://www.willms-edv.de
/--------- L.WILLMS@jpberlin.de -- Alle Rechte vorbehalten --

Die Wï¿½rter-Welt.  -G.C.Lichtenberg
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trim equivilant

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "Howard Brazee" <howard@brazee.net>
- **Date:** 2005-01-06T20:13:30+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<crk67e$1og$1@peabody.colorado.edu>`
- **References:** `<JKSdnQ34P4P1pkDcRVn-qw@massilloncabletv.com> <XcbDd.14271$7n1.950640@news20.bellglobal.com> <xLidnYbV_8L1zUDcRVn-pg@massilloncabletv.com> <9OMMq7xuflB@jpberlin-l.willms.jpberlin.de>`

```
I've used several solutions.    The programmer who has used other languages
might find his easiest solution to be reference modification.

PERFORM VARYING MY-INDEX FROM 79 BY -1
	UNTIL MY-INDEX < 1
                  OR MY-STRING (MY-INDEX) > SPACE
END-PERFORM
ADD 1  TO MY-INDEX
MOVE MY-STRING (80)     TO MY-STRING (MY-INDEX:).

*** Note the colon after the last MY-INDEX.  It means the TO is a string going
to the end of MY-STRING.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trim equivilant

- **From:** "JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2005-01-06T09:08:18-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<10tql33omp3ne62@news.supernews.com>`
- **References:** `<JKSdnQ34P4P1pkDcRVn-qw@massilloncabletv.com> <XcbDd.14271$7n1.950640@news20.bellglobal.com> <xLidnYbV_8L1zUDcRVn-pg@massilloncabletv.com>`

```
Rover wrote:
> I need to out put to a report a constant followed by a text field
> followed by a constant.  So if that text field has trailing spaces i
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> XkjhX

It's an easy task in COBOL, too, and there are several ways, but your 
question is still a tad unclear.

Nevertheless, in the most straight-forward case:

MOVE SPACE TO OUTPUT-FIELD.
STRING {constant1} DELIMITED SIZE
   {text-field} DELIMITED SPACE
   {constant2} DELIMITED SIZE
   INTO OUTPUT-FIELD.

The above presumes there are no imbedded spaces in {text-field}. If there 
are imbedded spaces in {text-field} but never two or more consecutive 
imbedded spaces, the line {text-field} DELIMITED 'bb' should be used instead 
(where 'bb' is two blanks).

There are other techniques, growing in complexity, that might be used 
depending on the anticipated nature of the data. Some involve wolverines.

You'll find that things that are trivial in one language are difficult in 
another - and vice-versa. For example, computing the hyperbolic arc tangent 
is tough in COBOL, but only one line of code in FORTRAN. Conversely, 
floating a currency symbol is piddly in COBOL but a lot of coding in many 
other languages.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trim equivilant

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "Chuck Stevens" <charles.stevens@unisys.com>
- **Date:** 2005-01-06T08:46:37-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<crjq10$28ut$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com>`
- **References:** `<JKSdnQ34P4P1pkDcRVn-qw@massilloncabletv.com> <XcbDd.14271$7n1.950640@news20.bellglobal.com> <xLidnYbV_8L1zUDcRVn-pg@massilloncabletv.com> <10tql33omp3ne62@news.supernews.com>`

```

"JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com> wrote in message
news:10tql33omp3ne62@news.supernews.com...

> Nevertheless, in the most straight-forward case:
>
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> imbedded spaces, the line {text-field} DELIMITED 'bb' should be used
instead
> (where 'bb' is two blanks).
>
> There are other techniques, growing in complexity, that might be used
> depending on the anticipated nature of the data. Some involve wolverines.

Presuming you've got a COBOL compiler that conforms to the '85 standard and
includes support for the Intrinsic Function Amendment, it's not all that
complex.  The program below illustrates the technique and should, so far as
I know, work just about anywhere:

000100 IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
000200 ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
000300 DATA DIVISION.
000400 WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
000500 01  INPUT-FIELD PIC X(50).
000600 01  OUTPUT-FIELD PIC X(100).
000700 01  NUM-ITEM-1 PIC S9(10).
000800 01  NUM-ITEM-2 PIC S9(10).
000900 PROCEDURE DIVISION.
001000 MAIN-PARAGRAPH.
001100     MOVE "EFG    H        I " TO INPUT-FIELD.
001200     INSPECT FUNCTION REVERSE (INPUT-FIELD)
001300         TALLYING NUM-ITEM-1 FOR LEADING SPACES.
001400     COMPUTE NUM-ITEM-1 =
001500         FUNCTION LENGTH (INPUT-FIELD) - NUM-ITEM-1.
001600     DISPLAY NUM-ITEM-1.
001700     MOVE SPACES TO OUTPUT-FIELD.
001800     STRING "ABC," DELIMITED BY SIZE
001900             INPUT-FIELD (1 : NUM-ITEM-1) DELIMITED BY SIZE
002000             ",JKL" DELIMITED BY SIZE
002100              INTO OUTPUT-FIELD.
002200     DISPLAY OUTPUT-FIELD.
002300     STOP RUN.

Note that Function TRIM has been proposed for the COBOL standard currently
in development and planned for publication in 2008.   The proposed function
accepts one or two parameters.  The first parameter is an alphanumeric or
national character data item; the second, if it is present, is a mnemonic,
"LEFT" or "RIGHT".  If the second parameter is absent, the function returns
the original string stripped of both leading and trailing spaces.  If the
second parameter is LEFT, the function returns the original string stripped
of leading spaces; if the second parameter is RIGHT, the function returns
the original string stripped of trailing spaces.  See J4/04-0198, accessible
from www.cobolportal.com/j4/ , for the latest distributed draft of the
proposal that includes this capability.

    -Chuck Stevens
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: 2008 vs 2009 (was: Trim equivilant

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** "Chuck Stevens" <charles.stevens@unisys.com>
- **Date:** 2005-01-06T13:29:14-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<crkaip$2k44$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com>`
- **References:** `<JKSdnQ34P4P1pkDcRVn-qw@massilloncabletv.com> <XcbDd.14271$7n1.950640@news20.bellglobal.com> <xLidnYbV_8L1zUDcRVn-pg@massilloncabletv.com> <10tql33omp3ne62@news.supernews.com> <crjq10$28ut$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com> <vdgDd.288876$lR6.49713@news.easynews.com>`

```
No; I hadn't downloaded 04-0182 onto my system or otherwise paid much
attention to it (in either its J4 or WG4 instantiations).   That's not been
my focus.

Once we get into the "alphabet soup" of WD, CD, FCD, DIS, FDIS, IS stages
for this document, perhaps it'll be easier to refer to it without dates
entirely.  J4 had hoped to have the WG in distribution by December; we're
now looking at next summer.

As it is, I don't think it matters much *in 2005*  whether the
*most-recently-revised draft schedule* for the development of the standard
calls for publication call for it in December 2008 or July 2009, given that
those *plans*, no matter how perfectly constructed the document is at the
time, might be knocked into a cocked hat by any of the many stages of the
review and approval process.

I know my focus has been on ensuring that the feature content of the
portions of that proposed standard is as complete and as well-wrought as I'm
capable of making it, and on time.  So far, I think the record shows I've
done a pretty good job of that.

Should I be devoting my energy instead to whether, and how well, projects
other than those for which I'm directly responsible are proceeding?

I don't much care what it's called at this point.  "COBOL standard planned
for publication in 2008" is too clumsy as it is.   I'd actually prefer the
"proposed COBOL standard" once it's reached at least formal Working Draft
status, but at the moment "the aggregate of all the proposals approved for
incorporation taken in combination with the base document for the informal
working draft of the proposed COBOL standard " is arguably more accurate
(though hardly as clear).  The Project Editor has not yet distributed an
informal working draft containing any of the technical proposals.  I'm also
of the opinion that many readers would think I was talking about "the
standard after ANSI X3.23-1985" if I said "the proposed COBOL standard".

My intent in referring to it the way I did was to *identify* what I was
talking about, not to specify, or accept personal responsibility for, when
the actual completed standard is made available by ISO or ANSI.

    -Chuck Stevens

```
.    On  06.01.05
  wrote  michael.mattias@gte.net (Michael Mattias)
     on  /COMP/LANG/COBOL
     in  yKhDd.5209$Xi1.2052@newssvr31.news.prodigy.com
  about  Re: Trim equivilant


>> I need to out put to a report a constant followed by a text field
>> followed by a constant.  So if that text field has trailing spaces i
>> must remove them.  In other languages that is an easy task (see my
>> note below).

MM> Yes, it's easy in BASIC, but for ten points, the refrigerator and
MM> whatever the hell is behind door #3, name one language OTHER THAN
MM> BASIC in which it's "easy."

   This question would be easier to answer the other way round: which  
language besides COBOL does not have a data type (explicitely or  
implicitely) of a STRING as something with a variable length, and this  
again explicitely or implicitely?

   Fortran is probably a prime candidate (I am omissing assembly  
languages). In C it is not explicit in the core of the language, but a  
number of standard library routines and the convention, that a NULL  
character terminates a string, help to it.

   In Pascal it is not in the original language, but Turbo Pascal and  
Delphi in its wake have introduced the concept.


Yours,

#### ↳ Re: Trim equivilant

- **From:** Robert Wagner <spamblocker-robert@wagner.net>
- **Date:** 2005-01-06T15:18:13+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<07kqt0d6ks9hsebha2778he1dmb0qbajbf@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<JKSdnQ34P4P1pkDcRVn-qw@massilloncabletv.com>`

```
On Thu, 06 Jan 2005 08:21:48 -0500, Rover
<rover44614@RemoveThisStuffNetscape.net> wrote:

>How do I strip (trim) trailing spaces from a text field?  In Visual 
>Basic that's newField = trim(textField).

[Bounds checking omitted for simplicity.]

01  original-string   pic x(9999).
01  truncated-string.
     05  b occurs 1 to 9999 depending on l pic x.
01  l    pic 9999.

Solution 1:
    move zero to l
    inspect reverse (original-string) tallying l for leading space
    compute l = length(original-string) - l
    move original-string to truncated-string

Solution 2:
    perform varying l from 1 by 1
        until truncated-string = original-string
       move original-string to truncated-string
    end-perform

Solution 3:
    move zero to l
    inspect original-string tallying l for '  '  *> look for 2 spaces
    move original-string to truncated-string

Solution 4:
    move length(original-string) to l
    move original-string to truncated-string
    perform varying l from l by -1 until b(l) not = space 
     continue end-perform
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Trim equivilant

- **From:** Rover <rover44614@RemoveThisStuffNetscape.net>

```
MOVE SPACE TO OUTPUT-FIELD.
STRING {constant1} DELIMITED SIZE
    {text-field} DELIMITED SPACE
    {constant2} DELIMITED SIZE
    INTO OUTPUT-FIELD.

#### ↳ Re: Trim equivilant

- **From:** LX-i <lxi0007@netscape.net>
- **Date:** 2005-01-06T18:22:46-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<wDkDd.1305$fR3.595@fe40.usenetserver.com>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<JKSdnQ34P4P1pkDcRVn-qw@massilloncabletv.com>`
- **References:** `<JKSdnQ34P4P1pkDcRVn-qw@massilloncabletv.com>`

```
Rover wrote:
> How do I strip (trim) trailing spaces from a text field?  In Visual 
> Basic that's newField = trim(textField).

This is a bit nit-picky, but trim(textField) will trim both leading 
*and* trailing spaces.  To only trim leading, there's ltrim, and for 
only trimming trailing, there's rtrim.

I see you've already received a plethora of COBOL solutions, so I'll not 
both with those.  :)
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Trim equivilant

- **From:** "Chuck Stevens" <charles.stevens@unisys.com>
- **Date:** 2005-01-07T08:55:12-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<crmesv$111b$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com>`
- **References:** `<JKSdnQ34P4P1pkDcRVn-qw@massilloncabletv.com> <wDkDd.1305$fR3.595@fe40.usenetserver.com>`

```
And to clarify and summarize, what's proposed for the next COBOL standard is
    TRIM (x, LEFT) eliminates leading spaces, leaving embedded and trailing
spaces;
    TRIM (x, RIGHT) eliminates trailing spaces, leaving leading and embedded
spaces;
    TRIM (x) eliminates leading and trailing spaces, leaving embedded spaces
alone.

    -Chuck Stevens

Chuck Stevens wrote:
> And to clarify and summarize, what's proposed for the next COBOL
>    standard is TRIM (x, LEFT) eliminates leading spaces, leaving
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>    -Chuck Stevens

I can visualize what TRIM(x LEFT) does. It left-justifies the data in the 
field.

Then I considered:
01  MY-DATA    PIC X(80) VALUE 'A'.

then

TRIM (MY-DATA RIGHT)

and my brain exploded.

What's now in MY-DATA if the trailing spaces are eliminated? Bananas? 
Weasels? The little grains of salt in the bottom of a potato-chip bag?

I have to go lie down.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trim equivilant

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "Chuck Stevens" <charles.stevens@unisys.com>
- **Date:** 2005-01-07T15:16:16-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<crn57e$1g2s$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com>`
- **References:** `<JKSdnQ34P4P1pkDcRVn-qw@massilloncabletv.com> <wDkDd.1305$fR3.595@fe40.usenetserver.com> <crmesv$111b$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com> <10tu4nc1pvg108e@news.supernews.com>`

```
The TRIM *function* does not change the value of the first *argument*.

Given MY-DATA PIC X(80) VALUE "A":

Regardless of the presence or value of the second parameter, what'd end up
in MY-DATA is what you started with -- "A" followed by seventy-nine spaces.
You have to *do something* with the function -- MOVE, STRING, whatever.

Presuming DESTINATION PIC X(100), and presuming also MOVE LOW-VALUES TO
DESTINATION before each of the following.

STRING FUNCTION TRIM (MY-DATA, RIGHT) DELIMITED BY SIZE INTO DESTINATION:
What I'd expect in DESTINATION is a single character "A" in the first byte
followed by ninety-nine bytes of LOW-VALUES.   Only one byte would have been
"strung".

STRING FUNCTION TRIM (MY-DATA, LEFT) DELIMITED BY SIZE INTO DESTINATION:
What I'd expect in DESTINATION is a single character "A" followed by
seventy-nine bytes of spaces, followed in turn by twenty bytes of
LOW-VALUES.  Eighty bytes would have been "strung".

Does that help?

       -Chuck Stevens

"JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com> wrote in message
news:10tu4nc1pvg108e@news.supernews.com...
> Chuck Stevens wrote:
> > And to clarify and summarize, what's proposed for the next COBOL
…[27 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

   Am I correct that the "temporary data item" created by the TRIM function will 
be a valid argument to the LENGTH (and BYTE-LENGTH) intrinsic functions?  e.g

   Function Length (Function Trim (My-Data))

would "yield" the length of the data between the first non-blank character and 
the last non-blank character?

- **From:** "Chuck Stevens" <charles.stevens@unisys.com>

```
I would want to amend both limits in your description to read "... character
inclusive".  Both the first non-blank character and the last non-blank
character are included in the result of Function TRIM; the way you specified
it might be read to indicate that the result starts with the character
*after* the first non-blank character and ends with the character *before*
the last non-blank character.

If the amended version is what you meant, then yes, that would be my
expectation.

    -Chuck Stevens

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trim equivilant

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "JJ" <jj@nospam.com>
- **Date:** 2005-01-08T22:02:26-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<D5WdnXwkaJVcA33cRVn-sw@comcast.com>`
- **References:** `<JKSdnQ34P4P1pkDcRVn-qw@massilloncabletv.com> <crmesv$111b$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com> <10tu4nc1pvg108e@news.supernews.com> <9OUZMvu9flB@jpberlin-l.willms.jpberlin.de>`

```
They make sense with any PIC X() variable, especially when used in a STRING.

For example (and basically the thing that started this whole thread), there 
is no quick way to STRING two PIC X() variables together, eliminating the 
trailing spaces, but preserving any embedded spaces.  It can be done, but 
not really consisely.

01 STR-1    PIC X(80) VALUE "ABC  DEF".
01 STR-2    PIC X(50) VALUE "GHI       JKL".
01 DEST    PIC X(130).

Now I want to STRING together STR-1 and STR-2 ignoring trailing spaces, 
resulting in "ABC  DEFGHI       JKL" in DEST.

It appears that I would be able to do:

   STRING FUNCTION TRIM(STR-1,RIGHT), FUNCTION TRIM(STR-2,RIGHT)
              DELIMITED BY SIZE
              INTO DEST

If they wanted to make my life even easier, I'd like to see something like a 
"WITH INITIALIZE" clause on the STRING, which would move spaces to DEST 
before doing the STRING.  In all my time programming in Cobol, I have never 
needed to keep the data in the destination of a STRING from prior to the 
string, except when using WITH POITNER, meaning practially every STRING is 
preceeded by a "MOVE SPACES TO".  (In fact, to be even more lazy, I'd like 
to see the compiler I use have an option to automatically apply this 
theoretical "WITH INITIALIZE" clause when "WITH POINTER" was not used.


"Lueko Willms" <l.willms@jpberlin.de> wrote in message 
news:9OUZMvu9flB@jpberlin-l.willms.jpberlin.de...
>.    On  07.01.05
>  wrote  nospam@bisusa.com (JerryMouse)
…[17 more quoted lines elided]…
> Das Buch mu� erst ausgedroschen werden. -G.C.Lichtenberg
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trim equivilant

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** l.willms@jpberlin.de (Lueko Willms)
- **Date:** 2005-01-09T06:31:00+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9OYg6qpuflB@jpberlin-l.willms.jpberlin.de>`
- **References:** `<JKSdnQ34P4P1pkDcRVn-qw@massilloncabletv.com> <9OUZMvu9flB@jpberlin-l.willms.jpberlin.de> <D5WdnXwkaJVcA33cRVn-sw@comcast.com>`

```
.    On  08.01.05
  wrote  jj@nospam.com (JJ)
     on  /COMP/LANG/COBOL
     in  D5WdnXwkaJVcA33cRVn-sw@comcast.com
  about  Re: Trim equivilant

   on the proposed TRIM() functions for the next COBOL standard:

j> They make sense with any PIC X() variable, especially when used in a
j> STRING.

j> For example

j> Now I want to STRING together STR-1 and STR-2 ignoring trailing
j> spaces, resulting in "ABC  DEFGHI       JKL" in DEST.
j>
j> It appears that I would be able to do:
j>
j>    STRING FUNCTION TRIM(STR-1,RIGHT), FUNCTION TRIM(STR-2,RIGHT)
j>               DELIMITED BY SIZE
j>               INTO DEST

   This is exactly what I said:

>>   I think that these TRIM FUNCTIONs make only sense with ANY length
>> items. Is that PIC ANY? Something like that. It has to be a datatype
>> which in and by itself is of variable length.

   the "DELIMITED BY SIZE" clause uses the SIZE of the sending item,  
and with the TRIM() function one treats it as one of variable length.


   As to this proposal by you:

j> If they wanted to make my life even easier, I'd like to see something
j> like a "WITH INITIALIZE" clause on the STRING, which would move spaces
j> to DEST before doing the STRING.  In all my time programming in Cobol,
j> I have never needed to keep the data in the destination of a STRING
j> from prior to the string, except when using WITH POITNER, meaning
j> practially every STRING is preceeded by a "MOVE SPACES TO".  (In fact,
j> to be even more lazy, I'd like to see the compiler I use have an
j> option to automatically apply this theoretical "WITH INITIALIZE"
j> clause when "WITH POINTER" was not used.

   I say NO, since you can easily code a MOVE SPACE TO ... before the  
STRING, and the STRING is explicitly for assigning values to  
individual positions in the receiving item, not for spreading SPACES  
to the rest of the item as is caused by the MOVE statement.


Yours,
Lï¿½ko Willms                                     http://www.willms-edv.de
/--------- L.WILLMS@jpberlin.de -- Alle Rechte vorbehalten --

Er liebte hauptsï¿½chlich die Wï¿½rter, die nicht in Wï¿½rterbï¿½chern vorzukommen pflegen. -G.C.Lichtenberg
```

>They make sense with any PIC X() variable, especially when used in a STRING.
>
…[25 more quoted lines elided]…
>theoretical "WITH INITIALIZE" clause when "WITH POINTER" was not used.

01  with-initialize value spaces pic x(10000).

string trim(str-1), trim(str-2), with-initialize
    delimited by size
    into dest
```

    If you have comments, corrects, deletions, or additions, 
    please send private email to:

    This FAQ answers such questions as (and addresses topics such as):
   
     - Where can I get a COBOL compiler? 
     - Is there a free COBOL compiler? 
     - Where can I contact... ?
     - What about OO COBOL? 
     - Books about COBOL
     - COBOL Tools
     - What can/should I post in the COBOL newsgroups?
     - What about USAGE? COMP? Storage for data in xyz format? etc?

 		Tutorials? Etc
   
   	 and much, MUCH more

- **References:** `<ohhSb.561$b77.1266@dfw-service2.ext.raytheon.com> <zKWdnexlvbKyyLvcRVn-uw@comcast.com> <joe_zitzelberger-76A5A2.14354321082004@knology.usenetserver.com> <E6UVc.28452$5s3.19593@fe40.usenetserver.com> <lgreenwa-498FD1.02164422082004@chiapp18.algx.net> <i8jhi0lnnfq33io492caoaq08ls9njuenm@4ax.com> <GoQWc.12222$3O3.11918@newsread2.news.pas.earthlink.net> <kvpoi05spu0odple6vp8gh2sh7hv0sdnro@4ax.com> <BM2Xc.221$W_5.28@newsread1.news.pas.earthlink.net> <71345c8d9e1ad8ea8678fc2c7c99dd15@localhost.talkaboutprogramming.com>`

```
"kprasann" <ashprav@rediffmail.com> wrote in message
news:71345c8d9e1ad8ea8678fc2c7c99dd15@localhost.talkaboutprogramming.com...
> Hello, I'm trying to abend a micro focus COBOL program in AIX. The COBOL
> program was actually migrated from mainframes and has a CALL to ILBOABN0
> IBM routine.

Why not exit gracefully instead? ABEND on mainframe is usually done to let
the operating system "roll back" all the data created in a JCL steam.

Of course, you *could* force an abnormal termination....

instead of CALL 'ILBOAAN0'  you could...

DIVIDE X BY ZERO

MCM
```

<ashprav@rediffmail.com> wrote:

>Hello, I'm trying to abend a micro focus COBOL program in AIX. The COBOL
>program was actually migrated from mainframes and has a CALL to ILBOABN0
>IBM routine.

call 'abort' using return-code

It will do the same as STOP RUN unless you set other options, such as
the one controlling memory dump.
```

- **References:** `<ohhSb.561$b77.1266@dfw-service2.ext.raytheon.com> <zKWdnexlvbKyyLvcRVn-uw@comcast.com> <joe_zitzelberger-76A5A2.14354321082004@knology.usenetserver.com> <E6UVc.28452$5s3.19593@fe40.usenetserver.com> <lgreenwa-498FD1.02164422082004@chiapp18.algx.net> <i8jhi0lnnfq33io492caoaq08ls9njuenm@4ax.com> <GoQWc.12222$3O3.11918@newsread2.news.pas.earthlink.net> <kvpoi05spu0odple6vp8gh2sh7hv0sdnro@4ax.com> <BM2Xc.221$W_5.28@newsread1.news.pas.earthlink.net> <71345c8d9e1ad8ea8678fc2c7c99dd15@localhost.talkaboutprogramming.com>`

```
Micro Focus (on some platforms) provide LE callable services.  Check for the 
existence of a CEE3ABD routine (which should be used on current mainframes 
instead of ILBOABN0 anyway)

I am not certain if the AIX products do or don't have the LE callable routines 
available.
```

<a id="t-8531d1571a"></a>
## Thread: Undocumented Fujitsu error message (another one...) — 23 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Undocumented Fujitsu error message (another one...)

- **From:** "Pete Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2005-01-08T01:02:00+13:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<347c20F48cb1mU1@individual.net>`

```
Has anyone ever seen a message come out of both PowerCOBOL and COBOL97
projects that appears as follows?:

NETWORK: NETWORK DRIVER APPEARS NOT TO BE SERVING THIS DIRECTORY

Why does it need a network driver on a standalone system? What directory is
it talking about? I can't believe how useless some of these messages are.

The project managers abort and it is not possible to compile or use COBOL,
as the project cannot be opened.(It MIGHT be possible to run these steps
manually, I haven't tried...)

I recently cleaned up my registry and obviously there was a setting there
that causes this if it is removed. Running Win XP Pro it should be a simple
matter to do a system restore and get back to where we were. I did this, but
all system restores have failed, and the result is that the system is left
unchanged.

I've checked the system paths and they are all intact.

I have no idea what this message is about. The system is currently not
networked and it makes no difference if I move the files to any other
directory, the same message appears.

I ran the repair facility of Enterprise COBOL, but it made no difference.

I don't want to have to uninstall and re-install COBOL because of all the
nonsense with transfering the license to a floppy and so on and, it is a
couple of years since I did it so I've forgotten how to go about it. (Can
probably read about it in Fujitsu notes).

If anyone can shed any light or give me a clue on this stupid meaningless
message, I'd be grateful.

Pete.
```

#### ↳ Re: Undocumented Fujitsu error message (another one...)

- **From:** "steve.t" <sthompson@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2005-01-07T06:32:29-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<1105108349.428853.23570@f14g2000cwb.googlegroups.com>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<347c20F48cb1mU1@individual.net>`
- **References:** `<347c20F48cb1mU1@individual.net>`

```
A friend of mine and I were just discussing these context sensitive
messages earlier this week. And you are correct, they are utterly
worthless unless you've had the problem before and have some idea about
it.

The new kids in development think that the xxxnnns or ssscccnns formats
are ancient and worthless (xxx being a three letter ID that matches the
component, nnn being a unique number of 3-4 digits and s being a
severity such as W/I/E/S. And then the sssccc being system and
component (seen in VM/CMS systems)).

Perhaps if more companies were aware of how difficult it is to diagnose
problems because the issuer of a message doesn't identify themselves,
they might force Micro$**t to do a better job with *all* their
software. But even better will be to force the OPEN world to do the
same thing! Try finding something like this in MAN pages!

Now more to your point, could it be that the way FJ goes about getting
to a certain folder it would appear to be a network read/write attempt
and so XP is complaining?

Otherwise, you may have to scan all the .exe and .dll files for the
majority of that message and then try to find why that particular
component is issuing it.

Regards,
Steve.T
```

#### ↳ Re: Undocumented Fujitsu error message (another one...)

- **From:** Donald Tees <donald@sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2005-01-07T09:38:42-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<QcxDd.29887$7n1.1181987@news20.bellglobal.com>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<347c20F48cb1mU1@individual.net>`
- **References:** `<347c20F48cb1mU1@individual.net>`

```
Pete Dashwood wrote:
> Has anyone ever seen a message come out of both PowerCOBOL and COBOL97
> projects that appears as follows?:
> 
> NETWORK: NETWORK DRIVER APPEARS NOT TO BE SERVING THIS DIRECTORY
> 

A WAG, but could it be a mapped drive or a CD?  I know in both cases. FJ 
does some wild and wooly things with drivers.

Donald
```

#### ↳ Re: Undocumented Fujitsu error message (another one...)

- **From:** "Thane" <thaneh@softwaresimple.com>
- **Date:** 2005-01-07T07:26:41-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<1105111601.029902.274340@f14g2000cwb.googlegroups.com>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<347c20F48cb1mU1@individual.net>`
- **References:** `<347c20F48cb1mU1@individual.net>`

```
Pete,

You may have stopped a necessary Crypkey service.  This is the service
running that manages the Fujitsu license.  You may need Fujitsu to
reset your key, revoke your license and reinstall.  It's possible JUST
a reinstall will do it as the Crypkey information should still be
there.  It may be that it's not because of the cleanup you performed.

Here's a link to a page offered by some other software about this
problem.  A Google search with your error message as the search
information is how I found this.
http://www.ceerelays.co.uk/PTW_FAQ/NetworkKey.htm
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Undocumented Fujitsu error message (another one...)

- **From:** "Richard" <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2005-01-07T10:42:04-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<1105123324.047711.249590@z14g2000cwz.googlegroups.com>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<1105111601.029902.274340@f14g2000cwb.googlegroups.com>`
- **References:** `<347c20F48cb1mU1@individual.net> <1105111601.029902.274340@f14g2000cwb.googlegroups.com>`

```
> You may have stopped a necessary Crypkey service. This is the service
> running that manages the Fujitsu license.

Pete, did you install XP SP2 exactly 30 days ago ?

If I recall, the FJ Cobol has a 30 day grace period before it requires
the licence.  If SP2 killed the mechanism then it may have thought it
was in that 30 days.

Have you installed the FJ Update Pack 2 which is required for XP SP2 ?
Perhaps that restarted the 30 day.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Undocumented Fujitsu error message (another one...)

- **From:** "Thane" <thaneh@softwaresimple.com>

Neither of these has an impact on the Fujitsu license.
```

Thanks Richard,

good advice. (As usual, from you :-))

However, I have NOT applied SP2 for a number of reasons.

I'm due for a new notebook (I try to replace them every three years as MTBF
on hard drives is usually around 10,000 hours; that is about 3 years for me)
and I expect the new machine will have SP2 already applied. That way I'll
have one machine with SP2 and another without.

Current environment is SP1 so the points you raised don't apply.

Thanks anyway,

Pete.

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Undocumented Fujitsu error message (another one...)

- **From:** "Pete Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz>

```
Thane,

thanks very much for this.

I have since found that it is certainly a key problem because I cannot even
use the ""Check registration" facility, let alone uninstall the software to
a floppy for transfer.

If I have to get Fujitsu to reset my key they may require an upgrade to V7
and I don't want to do that.

Raises an interesting point. I have a version I have bought and paid for. I
am not on maintenance/support (dropped it when I found there wasn't any...
During the period when I was sweating blood to get PowerCOBOL running DCOM+
and simply received undocumented messages and absolutely NO support from
Fujitsu...ended up rewriting the whole application in OO COBOL.)

I believe the company is morally bound to re-instate my current version, but
I have little recourse if they don't.

I'll try a re-install and see what happens.

If I have to go to Fujitsu for a new key, I'll report back here what
happens.

Thanks again for your advice. I didn't do a GOOGLE search, but maybe I
should have.

Cheers,

Pete.

>

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Undocumented Fujitsu error message (another one...)

- **From:** "Richard" <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2005-01-11T19:33:43-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<1105500823.715764.69900@f14g2000cwb.googlegroups.com>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<34j42lF4b2j7sU1@individual.net>`
- **References:** `<347c20F48cb1mU1@individual.net> <1105111601.029902.274340@f14g2000cwb.googlegroups.com> <34j42lF4b2j7sU1@individual.net>`

```
> they may require an upgrade to V7 and I don't want to do that.

Note that Fujitsu's UP2 is for V7 on XP SP2.  The list of what it
'fixes' implies that V7 won't run on SP2 without it. By extension nor
will V6.1.

I just checked that my version 6.1 still runs so it is not a general
expire date issue.

> If I have to get Fujitsu to reset my key they may ..

I don't see why they insist on upgrade to V7.  They should supply a key
to your existing version regardless of whether they support you or not,
there is no condition that voids your perpetual licence that you
purchased from them.
Still, it is useful to be able to have a 'backup copy' of the reg.
```

>> they may require an upgrade to V7 and I don't want to do that.
>
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
>Still, it is useful to be able to have a 'backup copy' of the reg.
Registry is not what matters here.

In order to allow a "usefull copy" of the instalation key you need to
use something like Norton Ghost to create a FULL copy of the HD you
install the license on.





Frederico Fonseca

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Undocumented Fujitsu error message (another one...)

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "Richard" <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2005-01-12T09:50:03-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<1105552203.133543.266710@f14g2000cwb.googlegroups.com>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<ecq9u0lmcgssicqq460fqrqmhpnepjacfh@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<347c20F48cb1mU1@individual.net> <1105111601.029902.274340@f14g2000cwb.googlegroups.com> <34j42lF4b2j7sU1@individual.net> <1105500823.715764.69900@f14g2000cwb.googlegroups.com> <ecq9u0lmcgssicqq460fqrqmhpnepjacfh@4ax.com>`

```
>>Still, it is useful to be able to have a 'backup copy' of the reg.

>Registry is not what matters here.

reg = registration.  I know it doesn't use the Windows Registry.

> In order to allow a "usefull copy" of the instalation key you need to
> use something like Norton Ghost to create a FULL copy of the HD you
> install the license on.

Partition Manager is a product that caters for backing up partitions.
One can have, say, two primary partitions of the same size (only one of
which is bootable), and a larger extended partition. PM can then do a
backup of the first primary to the 2nd.  If something gets corrupted
then the partition can be restored.
```

```
>>Still, it is useful to be able to have a 'backup copy' of the reg.

>Registry is not what matters here.

reg = registration.  I know it doesn't use the Windows Registry.

> In order to allow a "usefull copy" of the instalation key you need to
> use something like Norton Ghost to create a FULL copy of the HD you
> install the license on.

Partition Manager is a product that caters for backing up partitions.
One can have, say, two primary partitions of the same size (only one of
which is bootable), and a larger extended partition. PM can then do a
backup of the first primary to the 2nd.  If something gets corrupted
then the partition can be restored.
```

"JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com> wrote in message
news:10ua7mut590jqb4@news.supernews.com...
> Pete Dashwood wrote:
> > Thane,
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>

An interesting aside, but I already stated I have not applied SP2.

> >
> > Raises an interesting point. I have a version I have bought and paid
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> That's not exactly true.

Jerry, exactly what part of the above is not true?

I can assure you it IS true. The total lack of support from Fujitsu over
this is burned in my memory. I sweated blood to get this thing working, on a
customer site. It NEVER did. It just kept producing nessages that were
undocumented and which nobody at Fujitsu US had the faintest idea about. As
my customer was paying full maintenance and had gone with Fujitsu on my
recommendation, I was not amused. Finally, in desperation, I rewrote the
whole application as COM servers in OO COBOL. It then worked as documented.

I didn't dream the experience and I didn't make it up. It was a nightmare.

Neither am I in the habit of posting things here (or anywhere else for that
matter...) that are NOT true. (I accept that I may sometimes get things
incorrect, but not on this occasion.)

>With maintenance you are allowed to report, and FJ
> will acknowledge your report of, bugs in their software.

Oh Really? So you pay a full maintenance charge for the privelege of being
able to report bugs in the software (doing their QA for them), and they will
graciously say: "Thank you for your bug report" ?

Man, nice work if you can get it...

>"How to" questions
> and interaction with other software are fee-based.

And does "How do I get this software to work as documented?" constitute a
"How to?" question?

I did not come down in the last shower, and I rarely have problems with
Fujitsu software. In fact, it is a very good product that was not properly
or well documented in English. To be fair to them, Fujitsu have tried to fix
this and the current documentation is not bad.

It was not documented anywhere in the V6 docs that PowerCOBOL would have
problems with DCOM+. When I pointed it out and gave examples, they still
didn't accept it or acknowledge it, or fix it. The impression I received was
that their support staff had no idea what I was talking about. My only
recourse was to give up entirely on their "support", and skin the cat in a
different manner, which required many sleeepless nights and huge stress for
me.

Forgive me if I'm a tad testy about this; it still smarts...

>
>
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> You have other options. For example, there can be no reasonable
culpability
> if you take extraordinary measures to get a piece of software running that
> you paid for...
>
Er...I don't know why it STOPPED running. All I know is that nothing COBOL
development works. (COBOL applications continue to run, I just can't amend
them...)  I can't check registration, I can't re-install (probably can if I
uninstall, but I'm loth to do that), the repair facility runs and doesn't
fix the problem. I read and acted on Thane's post which related to a totally
different piece of software, and this indicates that the site code is
corrupted. I have the original site code but I can't re-enter it because it
won't let me transfer the license.

Fortunately, I'm not using COBOL at the moment (it would be really nice if
this happened during major development...). On the basis of this experience,
I am less likely than ever to use it. Who needs a development tool that
stops working for some unknown reason and produces an error message that is
pointless and useless, and, (as if that weren't bad enough) isn't documented
ANYWHERE by Fujitsu?

A less easy going man woul be really pissed off... <G>

Pete.
```

#### ↳ Re: Undocumented Fujitsu error message (another one...)- Solved -

- **From:** "Pete Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2005-01-16T11:27:40+13:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<34tjn4F4g39vtU1@individual.net>`
- **References:** `<347c20F48cb1mU1@individual.net>`

```
OK this is now solved.

There are a few lessons here so I'll summarise them as follows:

1. If you mess with the Registry, make sure you can restore it to the state
it was in before you messed with it.

(I know it's obvious, but I THOUGHT I could restore it, and later found
that the tool that was supposed to do that (WinXP Restore) didn't work...
 I used a tool called Registry First Aid. It is excellent and I decided to
register it. Before it runs it asks you if you want a snapshot saved and, of
course, I said "yes".

It detected that I was running Win XP pro and used the Operating System to
do the snapshot by creating a restore point.

The trouble is that this restore software is dangerous. It does incremental
restore points and if one of them can't be used for any reason, ALL of them
are useless. I had to get the facility working correctly before I could even
think about re-installing COBOL. Closing it down (suppressing restore
points) is supposed to delete all the restore points from a special system
folder called "System Volume Information". I decided to do this manually for
good measure, but couldn't because it is owned by the SYSTEM logon. I tried
booting into DOS but of course my file system is NTFS and, although I have
software that can see it, and copy it, it won't allow me to amend it. I
tried booting from a Linux CD but I didn't have enough knowledge of Linux
commands to delete the files, even though I could mount and see them.(It is
possible that it is limited to viewing NTFS only). So, finally I just had to
hope that windows had done it when I turned off the facility. (It had... but
I only found that out later.)

2. Don't defrag if you have Fujitsu COBOL installed.

I didn't actually DO a defrag as it wasn't required, but I believe just
opening this program MAY have caused my problem.


So, here's the problem and solution, summarised:

1. Ran Registry First Aid (RFA) and snapshotted a system restore point.
2. Checked for defrag; didn't need it.
3. Found COBOL development failed to work.(ALL programs...NetCOBOL,
PowerCOBOL, utilities, even the registration and registration
checker...nada, zilch.)
4. Tried restoring system from restore point in 1 above. Failed.
5. Tried the "Repair" facility of COBOL. It ran successfully but didn't
solve the problem.
6. Had a look at advice received in CLC, especially Thane's post. Looked
like a problem with the site key (which I had on file) but there was no way
I could get to enter it as registration utility wouldn't run.
7. Reluctantly decided to uninstall COBOL. Went to Control Panel> Add/Remove
programs... It failed. Could not uninstall.
8. Tried using uninstall from the original install disk. It grizzled about
some dlls it couldn't delete but it worked.
9. Re-installed from scratch. When it got to registration, tried using the
previous site code. (It was being re-installed on the same machine.) Failed
to validate.  Decided to ask Casper (the Fujitsu registration server which
is available 24/7) for another key. Casper replied within minutes (Good
stuff, Fujitsu!) saying he had no more keys and needed human intervention to
have more keys added to his database for this registration.
10. Paused for thought. Rebooted.
11. COBOL came back and said it was installed and registered OK! I did a
check registration and it said I was fine.

Everything currently works. IF it fails in 30 days, I'll get onto Fujitsu
and ask for another registration key.

I have a bad taste about this because I STILL don't know what the
undocumented message actually means, or what caused it, but at least my
COBOL development environment is back...

Hope this experience is of use to others.

Pete.

"Pete Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz> wrote in message
news:347c20F48cb1mU1@individual.net...
> Has anyone ever seen a message come out of both PowerCOBOL and COBOL97
> projects that appears as follows?:
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> Why does it need a network driver on a standalone system? What directory
is
> it talking about? I can't believe how useless some of these messages are.
>
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> that causes this if it is removed. Running Win XP Pro it should be a
simple
> matter to do a system restore and get back to where we were. I did this,
but
> all system restores have failed, and the result is that the system is left
> unchanged.
…[21 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

### Does anybody how to use the RANDOM function of VS COBOL?

- **From:** "HonestPerson" <AgiRic@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 2005-01-08T19:24:20+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ErWDd.2262$u47.1720@trnddc09>`

#### ↳ Re: Does anybody how to use the RANDOM function of VS COBOL?

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2005-01-08T19:56:10+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<uVWDd.410516$2W1.38145@news.easynews.com>`
- **References:** `<ErWDd.2262$u47.1720@trnddc09>`

```
What do you mean by "VS COBOL" (in the subject)?  If you mean either  IBM's

  OS/VS COBOL
or
  VS COBOL II

then the answer is that the INTRINSIC function "RANDOM" isn't valid for either. 
If you mean the "RANDOM" access method for Indexed or Relative files, then what 
don't you understand about it?

If you mean some other compiler, please tell us which compiler (exact compiler 
name and vendor) and operating system.

The first IBM mainframe compiler supporting intrinsic functions was IBM SAA 
AD/Cycle COBOL/370.  The only *currently supported* compiler for the MVS, 
OS/390, z/OS environment is Enterprise COBOL and it fully supports all '89 
Standard intrinsic functions.

NOTE:
  The CEERAN0 callable LE service - see:
http://publibz.boulder.ibm.com/cgi-bin/bookmgr_OS390/BOOKS/ceea3151/2.2.5.54

may NOT be called by either OS/VS COBOL or VS COBOL II - even if using the LE 
run-time library, but could be used from an Enterprise COBOL program.
```

I'm sure you've read these letters asking you to send $1 to six
different addresses. Putting your home address or mailing address on
the internet can be risky, but through PayPal all you have to provide
is your email address. It's safe and easy. Just go to www.paypal.com
and set up your FREE account! No credit card is needed. I am not a
gullible person and have never trusted things like this, but I gave it
a try. Hey it's only $6. I figured at least 6 people would respond
out of the millions of people that use the internet. You should give it
a try too! You have nothing to lose, and thousands to gain! Read on for
the letter that I found (of course, I added my PayPal e-mail on #6)...


HOW TO TURN $6 INTO $6,000+!!! THIS REALLY CAN MAKE YOU EASY MONEY!!!
IT WORKS!!! BUT YOU HAVE TO FOLLOW IT TO THE LETTER FOR IT TO WORK!!!!
A little while back, I was browsing through newsgroups, just like you
are now and came across an article similar to this that said you could
make thousands of dollars within weeks with only an initial investment
of $6.00! So I thought, "Yeah, right, this must be some kind of scam"
but like most of us, I was curious, so I kept reading. Anyway, it said
that you send $1.00 though PayPal to each of the emails stated in the
article. You then place your own email address in the bottom of the
list at #6, and post the article in at least 200 news groups. (There
are thousands) No catch. That was it. So after thinking it over, and
talking to few people first, I thought about trying it. I figured what
have I got to lose except $6.00, right? Like most of us I was a little
skeptical and a little worried about the legal aspects of it. It
follows the same regulations as the "mailed chain letters, which
according to the U.S. Post Office (1-800-725-2161) is indeed legal!
Then I invested the measly $6.00. Well GUESS WHAT!!? Within 7 days, I
started getting money in my PayPal account! I was shocked! I figured it
would end soon, but the money just kept coming in. In my first week, I
made about $20.00. By the end second week I had made a total over
$1,200.00!!! In the third week I had over $10,000.00 and it's still
growing. This is now my fourth week and I have made a total of just
over $36,000.00 and it's still coming in rapidly. It's Certainly worth
$6.00. I have spent more than that on the lottery!! Let me tell you how
this works and most importantly, why it works? Also, make sure you
print a copy of this article now, so that you can get the information
off of it as you need it.


$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$


REQUIREMENTS: You must have a confirmed PayPal account. If you do not
have a PayPal account you can go to www.paypal.com and follow the
instructions to set up a free account. In order to place the initial $6
into your account, you will have to confirm your bank account with
PayPal (which may take a few days). PayPal is 100% secure and is used
my millions of people world wide. Confirming a www.paypal.com account
simply means (1) PayPal will first ask you to confirm the email you
used to sign up your PayPal account by sending you an email with a link
for you to click to that email. As soon as you click that link your
email is confirmed. (2) PayPal will then make two small deposits into
your bank account if you set up your PayPal account with a checking or
savings bank account. Or PayPal will make two small deposits to your
credit card if you set up your PayPal account with your credit card.
Then after a few days (it takes up to a few days for these deposits to
appear in your bank account or on your credit card statement) then
after a few days you go back to the www.paypal.com website and sign in.
Then you click where it says this account is not yet confirmed. A
screen will pop up that gives you 2 boxes to fill in. You then enter
the numbers of the 2 small deposit amounts (two separate 2 digit
numbers) that were deposited to your bank account or credit card. Now
you have a working and confirmed PayPal account and can receive
payments.


$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$


[STEP 1]
Send, though PayPal, $1.00 to each email on the below list. Make the
subject of the payment "Email List" and in the comments, write "PLEASE
PUT ME ON YOUR EMAIL LIST." What you are doing is creating a service by
this - and best of all you are not giving your address to anyone you do
not know. THIS IS ABSOLUTELY LEGAL!! YOU ARE CREATING A SERVICE. Here
are the latest PayPal e-mails on the e-mail lists:


#1)brittany...@excite.com


#2) mth...@renc.igs.net


#3) Mishav...@aol.com


#4) nike31...@aol.com


#5) careent...@hotmail.com


#6) jjrvn...@hotmail.com


www.paypal.com


Subject of Payment: ***Email List***


Comments: ***PLEASE PUT ME ON YOUR EMAIL LIST***


[STEP 2]
Now take the #1) email off the list that you see above, move the other
addresses up one (6 becomes 5 &amp; 5 becomes 4, etc) the put YOUR
email address (the one used in your PayPal account) as #6) on the list.
**MAKE SURE THE EMAIL YOU SUPPLY IS EXACTLY AS IT APPEARS IN YOUR
PAYPAL ACCOUNT**


[STEP 3]
Change anything you need to, but try to keep this article as close to
original as possible. Now, post your amended article to at least 200
newsgroups, message boards, etc. (I think there are close to 32,000
groups) All you need is 200, but remember, the more you post, the more
money you make - as well as everyone else on the list! In this

then you will find the option to post a new message.

different addresses. Putting your home address or mailing address on
the internet can be risky, but through PayPal all you have to provide
is your email address. It's safe and easy. Just go to www.paypal.com
and set up your FREE account! No credit card is needed. I am not a
gullible person and have never trusted things like this, but I gave it
a try. Hey it's only $6. I figured at least 6 people would respond
out of the millions of people that use the internet. You should give it
a try too! You have nothing to lose, and thousands to gain! Read on for
the letter that I found (of course, I added my PayPal e-mail on #6)...


HOW TO TURN $6 INTO $6,000+!!! THIS REALLY CAN MAKE YOU EASY MONEY!!!
IT WORKS!!! BUT YOU HAVE TO FOLLOW IT TO THE LETTER FOR IT TO WORK!!!!
A little while back, I was browsing through newsgroups, just like you
are now and came across an article similar to this that said you could
make thousands of dollars within weeks with only an initial investment
of $6.00! So I thought, "Yeah, right, this must be some kind of scam"
but like most of us, I was curious, so I kept reading. Anyway, it said
that you send $1.00 though PayPal to each of the emails stated in the
article. You then place your own email address in the bottom of the
list at #6, and post the article in at least 200 news groups. (There
are thousands) No catch. That was it. So after thinking it over, and
talking to few people first, I thought about trying it. I figured what
have I got to lose except $6.00, right? Like most of us I was a little
skeptical and a little worried about the legal aspects of it. It
follows the same regulations as the "mailed chain letters, which
according to the U.S. Post Office (1-800-725-2161) is indeed legal!
Then I invested the measly $6.00. Well GUESS WHAT!!? Within 7 days, I
started getting money in my PayPal account! I was shocked! I figured it
would end soon, but the money just kept coming in. In my first week, I
made about $20.00. By the end second week I had made a total over
$1,200.00!!! In the third week I had over $10,000.00 and it's still
growing. This is now my fourth week and I have made a total of just
over $36,000.00 and it's still coming in rapidly. It's Certainly worth
$6.00. I have spent more than that on the lottery!! Let me tell you how
this works and most importantly, why it works? Also, make sure you
print a copy of this article now, so that you can get the information
off of it as you need it.


$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$


REQUIREMENTS: You must have a confirmed PayPal account. If you do not

into your account, you will have to confirm your bank account with

my millions of people world wide. Confirming a www.paypal.com account
simply means (1) PayPal will first ask you to confirm the email you
used to sign up your PayPal account by sending you an email with a link
for you to click to that email. As soon as you click that link your
email is confirmed. (2) PayPal will then make two small deposits into
your bank account if you set up your PayPal account with a checking or
savings bank account. Or PayPal will make two small deposits to your
credit card if you set up your PayPal account with your credit card.
Then after a few days (it takes up to a few days for these deposits to
appear in your bank account or on your credit card statement) then
after a few days you go back to the www.paypal.com website and sign in.
Then you click where it says this account is not yet confirmed. A
screen will pop up that gives you 2 boxes to fill in. You then enter
the numbers of the 2 small deposit amounts (two separate 2 digit
numbers) that were deposited to your bank account or credit card. Now

## Thread: CSQL-F-16:UNIX Exec error Return Code is 1035 — 7 msgs

### CSQL-F-16:UNIX Exec error Return Code is 1035

- **From:** "pradeep" <goelpradeep@gmail.com>
- **Date:** 2005-01-10T21:02:33-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<1105416898.263785.298610@f14g2000cwb.googlegroups.com>`

```
I am unable to compile Cobol program using MF Server express on AIX
server. The code compiles fine on Net Express Windows.

Below is our compilation directive file.

p(cobsql) csqlt=ORACLE8 dis sqldebug end-c mode=ORACLE PREFETCH=55
comp5=yes HOLD_CURSOR=NO sqlcheck=full Userid=regu34/autumn@dev01 p(cp)
sy endp
copyext(CPY) osext(pco,PCO) apost IBMCOMP list() WARNING ODOSLIDE
CONSTANT OS"UNIX"
CONSTANT NODAS"0"

Can any one help us.

Adios 
Pradeep Goel
```

>I am unable to compile Cobol program using MF Server express on AIX
>server. The code compiles fine on Net Express Windows.
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
>CONSTANT NODAS"0"

ORA-1035 means the precompiler lacks security privilege. Check
permissions on the userid of the compiler.
```

#### ↳ Re: CSQL-F-16:UNIX Exec error Return Code is 1035

- **From:** Robert Wagner <spamblocker-robert@wagner.net>
- **Date:** 2005-01-11T10:12:03+00:00

wrote:

>I am unable to compile Cobol program using MF Server express on AIX
>server. The code compiles fine on Net Express Windows.
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
>Can any one help us.

You can translate all Oracle error numbers to English by typing on the
command line:
oerr ORA 1035
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: CSQL-F-16:UNIX Exec error Return Code is 1035

- **From:** "Simon Tobias" <Simon.Tobias@microfocus.com>
- **Date:** 2005-01-11T13:33:32
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<cs0l7a$ud5$1@hyperion.microfocus.com>`

```
What happens if you invoke Pro*COBOL with the same options, i.e.

procob myapp.pco mode=ORACLE PREFETCH=55 comp5=yes HOLD_CURSOR=NO
sqlcheck=full Userid=regu34/autumn@dev01 ?

I suspect you'll see the 1035 error, as Robert alluded to.

SimonT.
```

#### ↳ Re: CSQL-F-16:UNIX Exec error Return Code is 1035

- **From:** "Richard" <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2005-01-11T09:54:49-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<1105466089.467297.259960@c13g2000cwb.googlegroups.com>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<1105416898.263785.298610@f14g2000cwb.googlegroups.com>`
- **References:** `<1105416898.263785.298610@f14g2000cwb.googlegroups.com>`

```
> sqlcheck=full

Oracle checks the SQL against the database when this directive is used.
If it doesn't have access permission (Userid=regu34/aut) then it is
going to give some sort of error.

Perhaps that AIX machine isn't in the list of machines allowed to
access, but the Windows machine is.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: CSQL-F-16:UNIX Exec error Return Code is 1035

- **From:** "Simon Tobias" <Simon.Tobias@microfocus.com>
- **Date:** 2005-01-11T18:46:48
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<cs17iq$k1$1@hyperion.microfocus.com>`
- **References:** `<1105416898.263785.298610@f14g2000cwb.googlegroups.com> <1105466089.467297.259960@c13g2000cwb.googlegroups.com>`

```

"Richard" <riplin@Azonic.co.nz> wrote in message
news:1105466089.467297.259960@c13g2000cwb.googlegroups.com...
> > sqlcheck=full
>
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> access, but the Windows machine is.

Alternatively, you may want to check your tnsnames.ora, which is located in
$ORACLE_HOME/network/admin , to ensure that your client is configured to
contact your server (dev01).

Either try using

tnsping dev01

or

sqlplus regu34/autumn@dev01

If those don't work, then I'd suggest either invoking Oracle's Network
Configuration assistant (netca), or copying the entry from tnsnames.ora on
your PC to the AIX tnsnames.ora.

SimonT.
```
